mainText:
Good morning Kurt ,  Tue Jun 23, 7:00 AM PT briefing:

Weather in Kenmore:
Now: 66 degrees Fahrenheit clear. High today 88 degrees Fahrenheit, overcast by afternoon, dry. Commute fine, no rain expected.
Heads up: Fri Jun 26 ,  85% chance of thunderstorm, high only 64 degrees Fahrenheit. Pack committee meeting is Fri evening ,  umbrella + jacket.

Calendar highlights for the next week:
Today Tue Jun 23, 7 to 8 AM: Dance (Kailey?)
Thu Jun 25, 1:15 PM: Dance + a "Testing Sat" entry at the same time ,  likely a test event to delete
Thu/Fri/Sat 10 PM to 2:30 AM: three "Testing Taekwondo" entries at Master Cho's Lynnwood ,  4.5 hrs at 10 PM is weird; probably test events. Worth confirming real vs. cleanup.
Fri Jun 26, 2:45 PM: Dylan appointment (body says "Dylan appointment 2:45pm" ,  medical/dental?)
Fri Jun 26, 7 to 8 PM: Pack committee meeting ,  Kenmore City Hall (scouts)
Sat Jun 27, 3 PM: Block party (15 min ,  looks like start of something longer, double-check)
Mon Jun 29, all-day: Cooking camp (Kailey?)

Emails that need your attention:
 OpenAI: "ChatGPT - Your data export has started" ,  sent yesterday 8:45 PM. This unblocks the Substack backlog (Lab Bench, Prioritizing, ESP32-S3, LVGL, About). Download link usually arrives in a few hours.
 Google: "Security alert" + "Google Verification Code" yesterday 8:42 PM ,  back-to-back. Could be a sign-in you triggered, or someone else trying. Worth a 30-sec check on your Google account activity.
Bank of America credit alerts (2) yesterday ,  credit balance increased + credit usage increased on one of your accounts. Informational; just confirms the monitoring is working.
Discover Card: new statement online (flagged). Routine.

Top three for today:
Check Gmail for the ChatGPT export download link (it'll come from OpenAI; check spam too). Once you have it, drop the ZIP and I'll list the conversation titles so you can pick which to import.
Decide on the Outlook drain continuation: <$0.25/day email budget means the v3 drain (52,200 queued IDs + 34,613 headers-with-no-move) needs a new plan. Options: (a) throttle to 1-2 batches/day, (b) pause until weekend, (c) just delete in bulk via Graph batch (no LLM cost ,  pure API).
One unblocked Substack win: Pi 400 + Pop!_OS outline. Source material already saved ,  I can draft an outline today, fully independent of the export. Easy momentum hit.

Personal dates coming up:
Nothing in the Jun 23 to Jun 30 window (no birthdays, no anniversaries). Dylan's 10th is Jul 26, Tricia's bday Sep 21 ,  both still ~1 month out, reminder engines already configured.

LLM spend
Yesterday (Jun 22): $3.70
Today (Jun 23): $0.52 so far

DAILY MAINTENANCE: Outlook junk-route job ran ok at 6 AM. Briefing job ran ok at 7 AM (this one).

Have a good one.
,  Annie
